skill.name: doc-coauthoring
skill.description: Guide users through a structured workflow for co-authoring documentation. Use when user wants to write documentation, proposals, technical specs, decision docs, or similar structured content. This workflow helps users efficiently transfer context, refine content through iteration, and verify the doc works for readers. Trigger when user mentions writing docs, creating proposals, drafting specs, or similar documentation tasks.
skill.summary: Structured workflow for collaborative document creation with three stages: Context Gathering, Refinement & Structure, and Reader Testing.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: All 18 static findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation co-authoring workflow that guides users through structured document creation. Tool references like `create_file` and `str_replace` were misidentified as external shell commands. Documentation type keywords (spec, proposal, decision) were incorrectly flagged as cryptographic algorithms. The single network reference to claude.ai is legitimate for reader testing. No actual security risks identified.

content.value_statement: Creating quality documentation is challenging. This skill provides a structured three-stage workflow that helps users gather context, refine content through iteration, and test documents with a fresh AI instance to ensure clarity for readers.
actual_capabilities: Guide users through three documentation stages: Context Gathering, Refinement & Structure, and Reader Testing
Ask clarifying questions to gather necessary context before drafting
Brainstorm and curate content options for each section
Iteratively refine document sections based on user feedback
Test document clarity with a fresh AI instance to catch blind spots
Suggest document structure based on document type (spec, proposal, decision doc)
limitations: Requires active user participation through multiple stages
Not suitable for quick edits or minor document changes
Does not generate content without user input and direction
Reader testing may require manual steps in web interface without sub-agent support

use_cases.title: Create Technical Specifications
use_cases.description: Draft comprehensive technical specs with proper structure, ensuring all necessary details are captured and the document is clear for implementation teams.
use_cases.target_user: Software engineers and architects creating technical documentation

use_cases.title: Write Decision Documents
use_cases.description: Develop decision docs that clearly present the problem, proposed solution, alternatives considered, and expected outcomes for stakeholders.
use_cases.target_user: Product managers and technical leads documenting project decisions

use_cases.title: Develop Team Proposals
use_cases.description: Create persuasive proposals that address stakeholder concerns, present compelling arguments, and include necessary context for approval.
use_cases.target_user: Team leads and contributors preparing team-wide proposals

prompt_templates.title: Start Documentation Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to create a [document type]. Can you guide me through your documentation co-authoring workflow?
prompt_templates.scenario: Initial trigger to start the documentation process

prompt_templates.title: Start with Template
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a document template at [URL/path]. Can you help me fill it in using your structured documentation workflow?
prompt_templates.scenario: User has existing template they want to use

prompt_templates.title: Improve Existing Document
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an existing document that needs improvement. Can we use your workflow to refine it and test it with a fresh AI instance?
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to improve existing documentation

prompt_templates.title: Full Documentation Project
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to create a comprehensive [PRD/technical spec/decision doc] for [project name]. The audience is [description]. Can we go through your full three-stage documentation workflow?
prompt_templates.scenario: Complete documentation project with full context

output_examples.input: I need to write a technical spec for our new API
output_examples.output:
I can help you create a technical spec using our structured workflow. Here's what we'll do:

**Stage 1: Context Gathering** - I'll ask questions about your API, its purpose, the technical approach, and who will implement it.

**Stage 2: Refinement & Structure** - We'll build the spec section by section, brainstorming content options and refining each part.

**Stage 3: Reader Testing** - We'll test the spec with a fresh Claude instance to ensure it's clear for implementers.

Would you like to proceed with this workflow, or would you prefer to work freeform?

output_examples.input: Help me create a decision document for adopting a new database
output_examples.output:
I'll guide you through creating a decision document for your database selection. Let's start with **Context Gathering**:

1. What type of document is this? (Decision doc, RFC, etc.)
2. Who's the primary audience?
3. What's the desired impact when someone reads this?
4. Is there a template or specific format to follow?
5. Any other constraints or context to know?

Please share what you know about your database options, the team evaluating them, and any specific concerns that should be addressed.

best_practices: Provide comprehensive context during Stage 1 - the more information you share, the better the guidance you receive
Use the iterative feedback loop - indicate specific changes rather than vague preferences for better results
Complete Reader Testing - testing with a fresh AI instance catches assumptions and gaps that are invisible to the author
anti_patterns: Skipping Context Gathering - jumping straight to drafting without sharing background leads to generic, shallow content
Providing minimal feedback - saying 'looks good' without specifics prevents the workflow from learning your preferences
Skipping Reader Testing - publishing documents without testing can leave critical gaps that confuse readers

faq.question: How long does the documentation workflow take?
faq.answer: The workflow takes as long as needed to create quality documentation. Simple documents may take 15-30 minutes. Complex technical specs or proposals may require multiple sessions.

faq.question: Can I skip stages if I'm in a hurry?
faq.answer: You can skip stages, but the output quality will suffer. If you skip Context Gathering, the content may miss critical details. If you skip Reader Testing, readers may find the document confusing.

faq.question: What document types work best with this workflow?
faq.answer: This workflow works well for technical specs, decision documents, proposals, PRDs, design documents, RFCs, and any substantial content that needs to be clear for readers.

faq.question: Do I need any special tools or integrations?
faq.answer: No special tools required. The workflow works with basic Claude access. Integrations like sub-agents (Claude Code) enable automated reader testing, but manual testing instructions are provided for web interface users.

faq.question: What if I have a document template already?
faq.answer: Share your template during Context Gathering. The workflow will use your structure and help fill in each section using the brainstorming and refinement process.

faq.question: How does Reader Testing work?
faq.answer: Reader Testing uses a fresh Claude instance (no context from your current conversation) to answer questions about your document. This reveals gaps and assumptions that authors overlook because they have all the context.
